milestone: Decoration Shapes -- real vector primitives with fill, outline, resize, and rotation
slice: An author asked why Builder had no shapes and, once told Studio's own Sticker Studio had a disconnected fixed-colour emoji Shapes category, asked for real ones: fillable, outlinable (thick/thin/coloured), resizable, rotatable -- a genuine new capability, not a quick fix, since rotation didn't exist anywhere in Engine V2 before this. Added five new namespaced Graphics-section fields (graphicShape/graphicFillColor/graphicStrokeColor/graphicStrokeWidth/graphicRotation) and a SHAPE_KINDS vocabulary (Circle/Star/Arrow/Speech Bubble/Banner) to the existing Universal Experience Content model, folded into the same Image/Graphics mirroring loop rather than a parallel mechanism -- a Shape is mutually exclusive with an uploaded image on the same Layer. The Decorations quick-picker gained a parallel "Add a Shape" tile grid creating a real Experience with graphicShape set directly (not rasterized like the emoji-glyph picker). engineRuntime.js gained a generic layer.rotation read plus a new _drawShape routine painting each kind as a real filled+stroked canvas path in both Working View and Runtime Preview. Reaching a published Theme needed one more fix, found in the same pass: the Scene-convergence Build pipeline had no path for a Shape Layer at all -- it would have silently compiled down to a stray generic sticker glyph in real Studio; fixed by converging layer.shape into a new decoration:{kind:'shape',...} Layer Pack entry and adding the matching kind==='shape' branch to renderer/slideRenderer.js's real Reader-facing drawing routine (kept in lockstep by hand, since that file has no dependency on any Builder module). Card/thumbnail previews across the Scene Stack sidebar, Experience Library, and Gallery/Nursery cards now show a shape-aware fill-colour swatch instead of a generic placeholder. Verified end-to-end via Playwright driving the real UI: creating a Star shape, editing Fill/Outline/Rotation through the real Inspector controls, confirming both canvases render it (Runtime Preview's sampled pixel matched the fill colour exactly), confirming image-upload correctly clears the shape on both the Experience and its mirrored Scene Layer, and running a real ProjectCompiler Build that compiled a correct shape decoration entry targeting the overlay scope. goldenBuild.js 30/30 unchanged. No Engine V2 Scene Model change; Frame's unrelated pre-existing Holder clip/mask Shape vocabulary (Rectangle/Rounded/Circle) was left untouched.
